title: petasites hybridus subsp. hybridus
intro: Petasites hybridus subsp. hybridus, also known as Butterbur, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its large, lobed leaves and purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the severity of migraines and other types of headaches. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, potentially reducing the frequency and severity of migraines. Butterbur is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is available in various herbal preparations, including extracts and dried leaves.
